title: Punishment for murder
description: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to a fine.

title: Attempt to murder
description: Whoever attempts to commit murder and does any act towards the commission of the murder shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term up to 10 years.

title: Punishment for rape
description: Whoever commits rape shall be punished with imprisonment of not less than seven years, but which may extend to life imprisonment.

title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
description: Whoever cheats and dishonestly induces the delivery of property shall be punished with imprisonment up to seven years and shall also be liable for a fine.

title: Punishment for theft
description: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with a fine, or with both.

title: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger
description: A magistrate can issue orders preventing unlawful assembly or activities that could cause public harm.

title: Examination of witnesses by police
description: Police have the authority to examine witnesses in connection with an investigation.

title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt
description: Whoever causes grievous hurt to any person shall be punished with imprisonment of up to seven years and a fine.

title: Punishment for rioting
description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of up to two years or with a fine, or both.

title: Criminal trespass
description: Whoever commits criminal trespass shall be punished with imprisonment for up to one year or with a fine, or both.

title: Defamation
description: Whoever defames another person shall be punished with imprisonment for a term up to two years or with a fine, or both.

title: Punishment for bribery
description: Whoever accepts a bribe or attempts to bribe shall be punished with imprisonment of up to three years, or a fine, or both.

title: Property obtained by cheating
description: Whoever dishonestly or fraudulently obtains property shall be liable for punishment under section 420.

title: Warrant of arrest
description: A warrant of arrest can be issued for the person charged with a crime.

title: Anticipatory bail
description: A person who believes that they will be arrested can apply for anticipatory bail.

title: Examination of witnesses
description: The police may examine a witness or person during an investigation.

title: Dowry death
description: If the death of a woman is caused by burns, injury, or any other unnatural cause within seven years of marriage, and it is shown that she was subjected to cruelty or harassment in connection with demand for dowry, it is considered a dowry death.

title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman with the intent to outrage her modesty shall be punished with imprisonment of up to five years and a fine.

title: Husband or relative of husband of a woman subjecting her to cruelty
description: If a husband or his relatives subject a woman to cruelty, such as harassment for dowry, they are punishable under this section.

title: Unnatural offences
description: Whoever voluntarily has carnal intercourse against the order of nature with any man, woman, or animal shall be punished with imprisonment for life or with imprisonment of either description for a term up to 10 years.

title: Causing death by negligence
description: Whoever causes the death of any person by doing any rash or negligent act shall be punished with imprisonment for up to two years, or with a fine, or both.

title: Power to postpone or adjourn proceedings
description: A magistrate can postpone or adjourn any proceedings for a reasonable time.

title: When bail may be taken in case of non-bailable offence
description: A person accused of a non-bailable offence may be granted bail by a magistrate if the accused is not likely to flee or cause further harm.

title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
description: Whoever dishonestly misappropriates or converts property entrusted to him for his own use shall be punished with imprisonment for up to three years, or with a fine, or both.

title: Enticing or taking away or detaining with criminal intent a married woman
description: Whoever entices or takes away or detains a married woman without her consent with a criminal intention shall be punished under this section.

title: Causing disappearance of evidence of offence, or giving false information to screen offender
description: Whoever causes the disappearance of evidence of any offence or gives false information to screen the offender shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of up to seven years and a fine.

title: Attempt to commit suicide by a person under eighteen years of age
description: Any person who attempts to commit suicide under the age of 18 shall be dealt with under the provisions of Section 107 of the Juvenile Justice Act.

title: Kidnapping or abducting in order to murder
description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person with the intention of causing their death shall be punished with life imprisonment or death.

title: Theft in a dwelling house
description: Whoever commits theft in any dwelling house, shop, or store shall be punished with imprisonment up to seven years and may also face a fine.

title: Cheating by personation
description: Whoever cheats by personation shall be punished with imprisonment up to three years, or a fine, or both.

title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in twenty-four hours
description: If an investigation cannot be completed within 24 hours of the arrest, the accused must be produced before a magistrate, who may authorize detention.

title: Withdrawal from prosecution
description: A public prosecutor or a magistrate may withdraw from the prosecution of a case with the consent of the court.

title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt using any dangerous weapon or means shall be punished with imprisonment of up to ten years.

title: Wrongful confinement
description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person shall be punished with imprisonment for a term that may extend to one year, or a fine, or both.

title: Forgery for the purpose of cheating
description: Whoever commits forgery intending to use it for cheating shall be punished with imprisonment for a term that may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to a fine.

title: Procedure for search under warrant
description: This section provides the procedure for conducting a search under a warrant issued by a magistrate.

title: Custody of person arrested
description: This section deals with the procedure for custody of a person arrested and the time within which they must be presented before a magistrate.

title: Punishment for false evidence
description: Whoever gives false evidence in a judicial proceeding shall be punished with imprisonment of up to seven years, and a fine.

title: Obscene acts and songs
description: Whoever, to the annoyance of others, sings, recites, or utters any obscene song, ballad, or speech shall be punished with imprisonment of up to three months or with a fine.

title: Criminal breach of trust
description: Whoever misappropriates or converts property entrusted to him for his use shall be punished with imprisonment for up to three years or a fine, or both.

title: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation
description: Whoever uses criminal force or assaults any person, other than in self-defense, shall be punished with imprisonment of up to two years.

title: Robbery
description: Whoever commits robbery shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of up to ten years, and shall also be liable for a fine.

title: Criminal intimidation
description: Whoever threatens to commit any offense punishable under the IPC with the intention to cause alarm to a person shall be punished with imprisonment up to two years, or a fine.

title: Sexual assault on children
description: Whoever sexually assaults a child shall be punished with imprisonment for up to ten years, and shall also be liable for a fine.

title: Power to direct police to register FIR
description: This section allows the magistrate to direct the police to register a First Information Report (FIR) if they believe an offense has been committed.

title: Cheating by personation
description: This section makes it a crime to impersonate someone else to deceive or cheat another person.

title: Personation of another person
description: Whoever falsely personates another, with the intent to deceive others, shall be punished with imprisonment for up to three years, or a fine, or both.

title: Punishment for criminal conspiracy
description: If two or more persons conspire to commit a criminal offense, they shall be punished with imprisonment of up to six months or more.

title: Non-attendance in obedience to an order from public servant
description: If a person fails to attend court or comply with an order of a public servant, they shall be punished with imprisonment of up to six months.

title: False information with intent to cause public servant to use power
description: Whoever gives false information to a public servant to make them use their power, shall be punished with imprisonment of up to one year or a fine.

title: Driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs
description: Whoever drives a motor vehicle under the influence of alcohol or drugs shall be punished with imprisonment of up to six months or a fine.

title: Fraudulent use of a document as genuine
description: Whoever fraudulently uses a document, knowing it to be forged, shall be punished with imprisonment of up to three years, or a fine, or both.

title: Attempt to commit suicide
description: Whoever attempts to commit suicide shall be punished with imprisonment of up to one year or a fine, or both.

title: Causing miscarriage without woman's consent
description: If anyone causes a woman to miscarry without her consent, they shall be punished with imprisonment of up to three years, or with a fine.

title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
description: If a person voluntarily causes hurt to another, they shall be punished with imprisonment of up to one year, or a fine, or both.

title: Punishment for theft in dwelling house
description: Whoever commits theft in a dwelling house, shop, or store shall be punished with imprisonment of up to seven years, and a fine.

title: Procedure for investigation
description: Section 157 allows for a police officer to start an investigation into any offense without the magistrate's approval, based on an FIR.

title: Power to require attendance of persons
description: A police officer may require any person to appear before them for an investigation into an offense if the person is believed to be a witness or suspect.

title: Punishment for assault
description: Any person who assaults another person shall be punished with imprisonment up to three months, or a fine, or both.

title: Husband or relative of husband of a woman subjecting her to cruelty
description: If a woman is subjected to cruelty by her husband or in-laws, they shall be punished with imprisonment for up to three years and a fine.

title: Word, gesture, or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
description: Whoever uses any word, gesture, or act to insult the modesty of a woman shall be punished with imprisonment of up to one year, or with a fine, or both.

title: Presumption as to abetment of suicide by a married woman
description: If a woman commits suicide within seven years of marriage due to cruelty or harassment, the husband may be presumed to have abetted the suicide.

title: Unnatural offenses
description: Any person who commits carnal intercourse against the order of nature shall be punished with imprisonment for life or a term of up to ten years.

title: Mischief
description: Whoever commits mischief (causing damage to property) shall be punished with imprisonment of up to two years or a fine, or both.

title: Abetment of act by public servant
description: A public servant who abets a crime shall be punished with imprisonment for a term that may extend to seven years and a fine.

title: Joining unlawful assembly
description: Whoever joins an unlawful assembly with the knowledge that the assembly is unlawful, is punishable with imprisonment for up to six months.

title: Refusing to sign a statement
description: A person who refuses to sign a statement or document as directed by a public servant shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of up to one month, or with a fine.

title: Disobedience to order duly promulgated by public servant
description: Whoever disobeys an order that has been duly promulgated by a public servant shall be punished with imprisonment up to six months, or a fine, or both.

title: Giving false evidence
description: A person who gives false evidence, intending to mislead a public servant, shall be punished with imprisonment for up to seven years, or with a fine.

title: Destruction of document to prevent it from being produced
description: A person who intentionally destroys a document that may be produced in a legal proceeding to prevent its use shall be punished with imprisonment for up to two years.

title: Refraining from complaint by a public servant
description: If a public servant fails to perform his duty in relation to a complaint or charge, he shall be punishable under law with imprisonment or a fine.

title: Punishment for wrongful confinement
description: Whoever wrongfully confines another person shall be punished with imprisonment for a term of up to three years, or with a fine.

title: Sexual intercourse against the order of nature with a minor
description: Any person who has sexual intercourse against the order of nature with a minor shall be punished with imprisonment for life or a term of up to ten years.

title: Forgery of valuable security or will
description: Whoever forges a valuable security, will, or any other document, shall be punished with imprisonment for life or for a term that may extend to ten years.

title: Defamation
description: A person who makes a defamatory statement intending to harm another person's reputation shall be punished with imprisonment for up to two years, or with a fine.

title: Criminal intimidation
description: Whoever threatens another person with injury to their body, reputation, or property, shall be punished with imprisonment for up to two years, or with a fine.

title: Confession before a magistrate
description: Any person who confesses to a crime before a magistrate shall have their confession recorded in writing, and it can be used in court.

title: Prosecution of judges and public servants
description: A judge or public servant cannot be prosecuted for any act performed during their official duties without prior sanction from the government.

title: Summary trial
description: If the magistrate is satisfied that the case can be disposed of summarily, the accused may be tried without a formal trial procedure.

title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
description: Any person who uses force on a woman with the intent to outrage her modesty shall be punished with imprisonment of up to five years and a fine.

title: Gang rape
description: If a woman is raped by two or more persons, they shall be punished with imprisonment for life or a term of up to twenty years.

title: Definition of Murder
description: Murder is defined as the unlawful killing of a person with intent to cause death or with the knowledge that death is likely to occur.

title: Power to proceed against other persons appearing to be guilty of an offense
description: If during an investigation, other persons are found to be involved, the magistrate can proceed with charges against them as well.

title: Bribery in elections
description: If any person is involved in bribery during an election, they can be punished with imprisonment for up to three years, or a fine.

title: Absconding to avoid service of summons or notice
description: A person who absconds to avoid serving a summons or notice shall be punished with imprisonment for up to one month, or a fine.

title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of duty
description: Whoever obstructs a public servant from performing their official duty shall be punished with imprisonment for up to three months, or with a fine.

title: False charge of offence made with intent to injure
description: If a person falsely charges another with a crime, intending to harm their reputation, they may be punished with imprisonment for up to three years.

title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
description: A person who voluntarily causes hurt to another shall be punished with imprisonment for up to one year, or a fine, or both.

title: Sexual harassment
description: A person who commits sexual harassment by touching or making sexual advances without consent can face imprisonment for up to three years.

title: Kidnapping or abducting with intent to secretly and wrongfully confine a person
description: A person who kidnaps or abducts with the intent to secretly and wrongfully confine a person shall be punished with imprisonment for up to seven years.

title: Kidnapping, abducting or inducing woman to compel her marriage, etc.
description: Any person who kidnaps or abducts a woman to compel her into marriage or illicit intercourse shall be punished with imprisonment for up to ten years.

title: Cheating by personation
description: Whoever cheats by impersonating another person shall be punished with imprisonment for up to three years, or with a fine.

title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
description: Cheating and dishonestly inducing someone to deliver property or money shall lead to imprisonment for up to seven years.

title: Mischief by injury to works of irrigation or by damaging or destroying public property
description: A person who commits mischief by damaging public irrigation works or property shall be punished with imprisonment for up to seven years.

title: Criminal trespass
description: A person who commits criminal trespass by entering or remaining on someone's property without permission can be punished with imprisonment for up to one year.

title: Lurking house-trespass or house-breaking by night
description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass or house-breaking by night shall be punished with imprisonment for up to two years.

title: Information in cognizable cases
description: The first information given regarding a cognizable offense must be recorded by the police officer in writing, which starts the investigation process.

title: Medical examination of the victim of sexual offense
description: The victim of a sexual offense shall undergo a medical examination by a registered medical practitioner as soon as possible.

title: Forwarding of accused to magistrate
description: The police officer, when arresting an accused, shall forward the accused to the magistrate within 24 hours.

title: False information with intent to injure a person
description: A person who provides false information with the intent to harm another person may be punished under the provisions of this section.

title: Power to discharge person in certain cases
description: A person can be discharged if the magistrate deems that no case has been made against the person.

title: Extortion
description: A person who extorts property, money, or services by threat of injury or force shall be punished with imprisonment for up to three years.

title: Adultery
description: Adultery occurs when a man has sexual relations with a married woman without the consent of her husband, and is punishable with imprisonment for up to five years.

title: Statements conducing to public mischief
description: A person who makes or publishes statements intended to incite public mischief or violence can be punished with imprisonment for up to three years.

title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
description: A person who intimidates another with threats of harm can be punished with imprisonment for up to two years, or a fine.

title: Public servant voluntarily violating laws
description: A public servant who willfully violates laws or orders relating to their official duties shall be punished with imprisonment for up to two years or a fine.

title: Illegal gathering of persons
description: If a gathering of people becomes unlawful, those involved may face punishment under this section, with imprisonment for up to six months.

title: Provocation with intent to cause riot
description: If a person provokes or incites others with the intent to cause a riot, they can face imprisonment for up to one year or a fine.

title: Punishment for committing affray
description: Any person who is found guilty of affray, which is a public fight or violence, can face imprisonment for up to one month, or a fine.

title: Detaining a person without a warrant
description: Detaining a person without legal authorization or a warrant shall lead to punishment for up to three years, or a fine.

title: Furnishing false information
description: Providing false information to any public servant in the course of their official duties is punishable with imprisonment for up to six months.

title: Refusing to answer public servant's questions
description: If a person refuses to answer the questions posed by a public servant during an investigation, they can face imprisonment for up to one month.

title: Issuing or signing false certificate
description: If any public servant signs a false certificate or document in the exercise of their duties, they can face imprisonment for up to three years.

title: False charge of an offence made to harm someone
description: If a person falsely charges another with a criminal offense, intending to harm their reputation, they can face imprisonment for up to two years.

title: Negligent conduct with respect to machinery
description: If a person negligently operates a machine or apparatus that causes harm to another person, they may face imprisonment for up to six months or a fine.

title: Extortion by threat of accusation of an offence
description: Extortion of property by threatening to accuse a person of a crime may result in imprisonment for up to seven years.

title: Dacoity
description: When five or more persons are involved in a robbery, it is known as dacoity and can lead to life imprisonment or imprisonment for up to ten years.

title: Cheating by false promise to deliver property
description: If someone makes a false promise to deliver property to another person with the intent to cheat, they can be punished with imprisonment for up to five years.

title: Punishment for criminal intimidation by threat of injury
description: If a person intimidates someone with a threat of harm or injury, they can face imprisonment for up to two years.

title: Word, gesture, or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
description: A person who uses words or gestures intended to insult a woman's modesty may face imprisonment for up to three years.

title: Duty to assist police
description: A person who knows about the commission of a crime must inform the police and assist in the investigation. Failure to do so is punishable under the law.

title: Arrest without warrant
description: In certain cases, the police can arrest a person without a warrant if they have sufficient grounds to believe that a crime has been committed.

title: Search warrant
description: A magistrate may issue a search warrant when there are reasonable grounds to believe that evidence of a crime exists at a particular location.

title: Order for maintenance of wives, children, and parents
description: A magistrate may issue an order requiring a person to pay maintenance to their wife, children, or parents, if they are unable to support themselves.

title: Procedure for contempt of court
description: If a person commits contempt of court, they may be punished under the provisions of this section.

title: Punishment for assault or use of force
description: Any person who uses force or commits an assault against another individual can be punished with imprisonment or a fine.

title: Power to arrest persons under warrant
description: A police officer may arrest a person if there is a warrant for their arrest issued by the magistrate.

title: Saving of inherent powers of the High Court
description: The High Court may exercise its inherent powers to make orders necessary to meet the ends of justice or to prevent abuse of process.

title: Collecting arms with the intention of waging war
description: Anyone who collects arms or ammunitions with the intent of waging war against the government can be punished with imprisonment for life.

title: Punishment for rioting
description: A person involved in a riot shall be punished with imprisonment of up to two years, or a fine, or both.

title: Rioting, armed with a deadly weapon
description: If a person commits rioting while being armed with a deadly weapon, they can be imprisoned for up to three years, with or without a fine.

title: Promoting enmity between different groups
description: Anyone who promotes enmity or hatred between different groups of people based on religion, race, or language can be punished with imprisonment for up to three years.

title: Punishment for participating in an affray
description: Anyone who participates in an affray (violent public fight) may face up to one month's imprisonment or a fine.

title: Unlawful detention
description: A person who unlawfully detains another individual may be imprisoned for up to one year or be fined.

title: Intentional causing harm to others' property
description: A person causing damage to property with the intent to harm another person may be subject to punishment, including imprisonment for up to two years.

title: Culpable homicide not amounting to murder
description: Culpable homicide not amounting to murder, such as causing death through negligence or without premeditation, may result in imprisonment of up to ten years.

title: Exposure and abandonment of a child under twelve years
description: A person who abandons a child under twelve years of age may face imprisonment of up to seven years.

title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
description: If someone causes grievous injury to another by using dangerous weapons, they may face imprisonment of up to ten years.

title: Assault or criminal force with intent to outrage a woman's modesty
description: Anyone using force or assaulting a woman with the intent to outrage her modesty can face imprisonment for up to five years.

title: Theft
description: A person found guilty of theft, which involves dishonestly taking someone else's property, may face imprisonment for up to three years.

title: Criminal trespass
description: Anyone who unlawfully enters someone else's property without permission can face imprisonment for up to three months or a fine.

title: Forgery for the purpose of cheating
description: If a person forges documents with the intent to cheat, they may face imprisonment for up to seven years.

title: Criminal intimidation
description: A person who intimidates another person with threats of harm or injury may face imprisonment of up to two years or a fine.

title: Word or gesture intended to insult the modesty of a woman
description: Any word or gesture intended to insult the modesty of a woman can result in imprisonment of up to three years.

title: Criminal conspiracy
description: When two or more people form a conspiracy to commit a crime, they can be punished with imprisonment of up to six years.

title: Voluntarily causing hurt to deter public servant from their duty
description: Anyone who causes harm to a public servant with the intent to stop them from performing their duty may face up to three years' imprisonment.

title: Power to remand accused to custody
description: A magistrate has the authority to remand an accused person to custody if the case is under investigation.

title: Compromise between parties in certain criminal cases
description: In certain cases, a compromise can be reached between the victim and the accused in a criminal matter.

title: Place of trial
description: This section provides the criteria for determining the place of trial for criminal cases, which depends on where the offense was committed.

title: Examination of complainant
description: A magistrate must examine the complainant in a criminal case, particularly when the case involves offenses under the Indian Penal Code.

title: Criminal conspiracy
description: When two or more individuals conspire to commit a criminal act, they are guilty of criminal conspiracy, which is punishable under this section.

title: Waging, or attempting to wage, war against the government of India
description: Waging or attempting to wage war against the government of India can result in life imprisonment or the death penalty.

title: Collecting arms, etc., with the intention of waging war
description: If anyone collects arms with the intent of waging war against the government, they will be punished with imprisonment for life.

title: Concealing with intent to facilitate the waging of war
description: Concealing weapons or plans to facilitate war is punishable with imprisonment for life.

title: Sedition
description: Anyone attempting to bring hatred or contempt towards the government or inciting violence against it is guilty of sedition, punishable with life imprisonment or death.

title: Dishonour of cheque for insufficiency of funds
description: If a cheque is dishonored due to insufficient funds, the drawer can be punished with imprisonment up to two years or a fine.

title: Personating a public servant
description: Impersonating a public servant or attempting to assume their authority is punishable with imprisonment of up to two years.

title: False information given in court
description: Giving false information or fabricating evidence in court can lead to imprisonment for up to seven years.

title: Intentional omission to give information of offense
description: A person who knowingly fails to inform the authorities of an offense can face imprisonment of up to three years.

title: Counterfeiting coins
description: Counterfeiting coins or producing fake currency is a serious offense and is punishable under this section.

title: Rape
description: Rape is punishable under section 375 of the IPC with imprisonment ranging from seven years to life or death, depending on the severity of the crime.

title: Criminal intimidation
description: If a person threatens another person with harm to their body, property, or family, they are guilty of criminal intimidation and can be sentenced to imprisonment for up to two years.

title: Warrant of arrest in case of non-bailable offense
description: In case of a non-bailable offense, a warrant for arrest may be issued by a magistrate or the court.

title: Warrant to search and seize property
description: This section allows the police or authority to seize evidence or property related to a criminal investigation through a valid search warrant.

title: Bond for good behavior
description: A magistrate can require a person to furnish a bond for good behavior to prevent potential disturbance or harm.

title: Examination of witness by police
description: This section mandates that any statement made by a witness to the police be recorded in writing.

title: Procedure when an accused is not released on bail
description: If a person is not granted bail, the police may seek the approval of a magistrate to continue their detention.

title: Procedure to be followed when a juvenile is arrested
description: When a juvenile is arrested, the police must follow a specific procedure, and the juvenile should be kept in a juvenile home or institution.

title: Procedure for obtaining an arrest warrant
description: If the police require an arrest warrant for a person, they must submit an application to a magistrate.

title: Arrest to prevent commission of cognizable offense
description: If the police have reason to believe that a person is about to commit a cognizable offense, they have the authority to arrest the individual without a warrant.

title: Causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others
description: Causing hurt by a dangerous act that endangers someone's life or personal safety, punishable with imprisonment or fine.

title: Causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety of others
description: Causing grievous hurt to another person through an act that endangers their life or personal safety.

title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
description: Assault or use of criminal force on a woman with the intent of outraging her modesty, punishable with imprisonment.

title: Kidnapping for ransom
description: Kidnapping a person with the intent to demand a ransom is punishable with life imprisonment or the death penalty.

title: Punishment for extortion
description: Extortion is when someone wrongfully obtains money or property through force or threats, punishable under this section.

title: Robbery
description: Robbery is taking property by force or the threat of force, punishable by imprisonment and fines.

title: Attempt to commit robbery or dacoity
description: Attempting robbery or dacoity with the use of a weapon or during a violent act is punishable under IPC 398.

title: Dishonest or fraudulent removal or concealment of property
description: Fraudulent or dishonest removal or concealment of property to prevent its recovery or disposal.

title: Mischief
description: Committing mischief involves willfully causing damage to property or any destruction of property.

title: Criminal trespass
description: Entering or remaining unlawfully on someone's property with the intent to commit a crime.

title: Forgery
description: Forging a document, seal, or electronic record to mislead or deceive, punishable under this section.

title: Forgery for purpose of cheating
description: Forging a document or seal with the intent to cheat is punishable with imprisonment and/or fine.

title: Husband or relative of husband of a woman subjecting her to cruelty
description: Cruelty or harassment by the husband or his relatives towards the wife can result in imprisonment under this section.

title: Intentional insult with intent to provoke breach of the peace
description: Anyone who intentionally insults or provokes another person to cause a breach of the peace may face imprisonment under IPC 504.

title: Criminal intimidation
description: Criminal intimidation involves threatening someone with harm to their body, property, or reputation.

title: Summons to produce documents
description: A summons may be issued to produce documents or evidence that are crucial to an investigation or trial.

title: Order for maintenance of wives, children, and parents
description: This section allows a magistrate to order a person to provide maintenance to their wife, children, or parents if they are unable to maintain themselves.

title: Inquiry by Magistrate into cause of death
description: A magistrate is required to conduct an inquiry into the cause of death when there is suspicion about the circumstances.

title: Power to arrest without warrant
description: This section grants law enforcement the authority to arrest an individual without a warrant if they are involved in a cognizable offense.

title: Power to examine the accused
description: A magistrate may examine the accused during a trial to provide their defense and explanation.

title: Power to release on bail
description: A magistrate may grant bail to an accused person based on the severity of the offense and the risk of flight.

title: Examination of complainant
description: A complainant may be examined by a magistrate to verify the validity of a case before issuing a warrant.

title: Procedure when a police officer is unable to complete investigation
description: If the police cannot complete an investigation within 24 hours, they must report to a magistrate, who may extend the detention period.

title: Abetment of a thing
description: If a person abets the commission of an offense by another person, they can be charged with abetment under this section.

title: Punishment for abetment
description: This section provides the punishment for a person who abets the commission of an offense.

title: Criminal conspiracy
description: A person is said to commit criminal conspiracy when they agree with others to commit an offense.

title: Punishment for unlawful assembly
description: Unlawful assembly is defined as an assembly of five or more persons with the intent to commit a crime or disturb public peace.

title: Joining unlawful assembly armed with deadly weapon
description: Joining an unlawful assembly while being armed with a deadly weapon is a criminal offense under IPC 144.

title: Promoting enmity between different groups on grounds of religion, race, etc.
description: This section punishes promoting enmity or hatred between different religious, racial, or other groups.

title: Confession made before a Magistrate
description: A confession made before a magistrate is recorded in writing, and if voluntary, can be used as evidence in court.

title: False statement made to a public servant
